rationale: Both candidates engage with the two ground-truth concerns (regression tests added, CuMemTag enum introduced) to similar depth. The decisive difference is the latent gap: Y pinpoints five pre-existing tests (sleep(level=2) followed by wake_up()) that will now raise NotImplementedError due to the new guard — exactly the over-strictness that broke merge CI per #4905/#4912 — with precise file/line citations and fixes. X's finding #1 gestures at the same mechanism via the sleep() default level but never ties it to concrete failing call sites, making it a weaker, more speculative version of the same insight. Y's findings are also more tightly grounded (explicit grep validations, exact line numbers per broken test) versus some of X's less-verifiable claims (e.g. worker-side default citations, allocator tag semantics).
